rejections.claim: Older adult with rheumatic heart disease, severe mitral and aortic stenosis, severe tricuspid regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, and prior CHF, presenting during an episode of hyperkalemia, hyponatremia, and hypocalcemia. The regimen across discharge and the POD 28 change includes furosemide, ramipril, spironolactone (as aldactone and within a furosemide/spironolactone combination product), digoxin, and amiodarone, alongside a potassium-rich diet of 7-8 bananas daily for the prior 3 weeks. Serum potassium was 7.87 meq/L and after treatment 4.5 meq/L; serum sodium 129 mmol/L; serum creatinine 1.1 mg%; BUN 17 mg/dl. No allergies are recorded. Several medications (metoprolol, amiodarone, ecosprin, acenocoumarol) have uncertain continuation status, and there are duplicate spironolactone- and furosemide-containing entries in the POD 28 regimen.
